name: Janice's Sweet Rolls for ABM
recipeInstructions:
Add to the bread machine in order preferred by your machine. (In mine, start with the flour and add dry ingredients first). Place yeast in the yeast dispenser.
Run this on your dough cycle. After completion, they can be finished one of several ways:
1. Take a pinch of dough about the size of a small egg, pull it, dip in melted butter, run through sugar/cinnamon mixture, and tie them in a knot.
2. Roll out to about the size of a cookie sheet, spread butter (slightly less than a stick) over the surface, then top with sugar/cinnamon mixture. Roll and slice about 1/2 inch thickness. You should get enough for about 3 9-inch round or square pans. Cover the bottom of pans lightly with melted butter (just a small amount).
Allow room for them to double in size!
Place in an oven set to 250°F for 2 minutes, then turn off the oven and place rolls in the off oven. Let rise until very light and doubled in bulk (about 1 1/2 hours). Cover with greased waxed paper. Do not use towels as they stick.
Bake in a preheated oven at 375°F for about 20 minutes. Remove from pan immediately.
Can be frozen. Best when eaten warm! The recipe will work as a caramel roll or even just a plain sweet dinner-like roll. Do not use too much butter as they will not rise enough!
